original text:
In order to build a corporate culture and forge bonds with your partners and customers, we always believe that people need to be part of a common event.
The success of any event organized in France lies in the commitment to running the event.
The outcome of an event will be successful if the event planner inculcates professionalism and expertise skills. The event planner is expected to have a sense of responsibility.
Thus he should use a distinctive, innovative approach that strives to add value to the event.
The budget is an overall determinant of the event quality.
1.	WНАТ ТYРЕ ОF ЕVЕΝТ?
An event planner can't organize an event successfully without knowing the type of event.
The event planner needs to know if the corporate event is a meeting, a conference, a business fair or a product launch. It could equally be an alumni event or a recruitment fair.
Whatever the case, knowing the exact nature of the event will enable the event planner to automate manual tasks. Such knowledge will also help to estimate the budget thereby facilitating preparations across the various departments.
2.	WНАТ RЕGІОΝЅ НАЅ YОUR РRЕFЕRЕΝСЕ?
If you want to host an event in France, it is also essential to know the right region.
Paris and Riviera-Monaco are some of the best destinations to host events in France. The corporate event planner or French DMC still needs to make a decision on where the event will take place. He may decide that the event will hold at the heart of a prestigious hotel, in a boat or even on the roof of a restaurant.
You should take note that France and most of its big cities have unique event sites. However, an exceptional opportunity to invite professionals for a meeting or workshop could change the scene. It is advisable to choose service providers in the city where the event will hold. For instance, if you settle for Paris, all your service providers will likely come from there too. It's cheaper this way and it's easy to estimate a budget after choosing the preferred region.
We believe that France is one of the best destinations in the world. The diversity of cities, landscapes, history and gastronomy are strong motto for a majority of our clients.
3.	WНАТ ЅТYLЕ ОF VЕΝUЕ?
France is a multicultural country so selecting a venue style for an event is quite easy. France offers a range of modern, traditional, and even trendy event styles. Therefore, the event planner should reach out to vendors and suppliers as soon as he books the venue. Arranging a kick-off meeting with these people will prevent last minute effort duplications.
Also, last minute arrangements can cost extra if the vendors are forced to adjust their schedules to accommodate your needs. It is imperative to choose an event style that matches your budget. You should not opt for a very high style which you can't afford. Also, ensure that everything is available for the event dates. Once you make the decision about the style, it will be easy for you to draw its budget.
4.	WНАТ РЕRІОD ОF ТНЕ YЕАR DО YОU РLАΝ ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ?
The period of the year is another crucial factor to put into considering while estimating the budget for an event in France. France has warm and cold seasons, and the event manager can advise which period is suitable for a particular event.
If you are planning an event in Paris, it's better to do it between June and October. The reason is that this period is relatively warm and your guests will be comfortable. Also, summer activities are quite different from winter activities. Therefore different seasons will probably have different costs.
Each period has his key features. The local Dmc is your best adviser to select the appropriate destination regarding the period of the year.
5.	НОW LОΝG ЅНОULD ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ ВЕ?
The duration of the event is a crucial factor to consider when planning an event in France. The length of the event will help the event organizer to better plan for the event. Knowledge of the duration will also guide you in drawing a practical budget for the event.
It is advisable for the French DMC to check out affordable venues and adjust dates to minimize cost. More so, the event planner can play with the event date. For example, you could start the conference in the afternoon so that you don't spend on breakfast and lunch.
6.	DО YОU RЕQUІRЕ АССОМОDАТІОΝ?
Accommodation is another important consideration to make when drawing a budget for an event in France. You need to decide whether or not you will provide accommodation for your guests. You might want to minimize cost by opting for twin bedrooms in the hotel.
The advantage is that two attendees get to share a single room. Whatever the case, just have in mind that many hotels in France offer very competitive and attractive group rates.
The majority of corporates go for 4 star properties.
You will find a larger choice and the best deals for groups in this category.
7.	НОW МАΝY РЕОРLЕ ТО ВUDGЕТ?
Knowledge of the number of people you are expecting will help you come up with a reasonable budget. If you are expecting many people, you can try to negotiate a package price for certain services. For instance, when it comes to transporting your guests, you may ask to be billed per bus instead of per person.
You might also opt for vans instead of buses. Such attitudes could help you reduce expenditures. The event planner should also be able to compare the costs per person and the expenditures per groups of attendees.
8.	DО YОU REQUIRE А ЅІТЕ VІЅІТ?
А sіtе іnsресtіоn іs thе bеst wау tо mаkе уоur fіnаl dесіsіоn.
The visits are organized by the local dmc to potential event venues.
The purpose of the visits is to check if the hotel and venues facilities are up to the standards for your corporate event.
Usuаllу, thе sіtе іnsресtіоn tаkеs оnе оr twо dауs. Vеndоrs аррlу dіsсоunt rаtеs for accommodation and transportation if you stay longer.
9.	НОW СRЕАТІVЕ АΝD ТАІLОR МАDЕ ЅНОULD ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ ВЕ?
Wе knоw thаt уоu wаnt thе bеst еvеnt оut thеrе, but thе rеаlіtу іs thаt nоt еvеrуоnе іs ехресtіng а mаstеrріесе. Ѕоmеtіmеs thе еvеnt јust hаs tо wоrk.
Hence, the French DMC may bring suggestions that will cause thrills and excitements. But you should have in mind that such creative ideas always increase the overall cost.
We know that you want the best event out there, but the reality is that not everyone is expecting a masterpiece.

original text:
Today we highly value personal care, but until the early 18th century, a man could in his life never to dive into the water. Many people believed that bathing - this is an unhealthy thing, and "soak" his body in water, especially hot, allows various diseases and infections to enter the body. Even if people still like to take a bath, he did it in the clothes! This habit persisted until the late 19th century.
Until the 1880s people did not think about creating a deodorant, despite the fact that most of them most of the time had very bad smell, but no discomfort from this, they have not experienced. Rich tried to mask the odor lots of spirits, but today, everyone knows that this method does not work.
It is worth noting that even in the 9th century scholar Ziryab (Ziryab) proposed the idea of ​​a deodorant for underarms, but it did not take. Up until 1888 there was nothing like that. That was the year has begun mass production of the first deodorant that had phenomenal success in the west. These deodorants are available today and are marketed under the brand name "Mom".
Women did not care for the hair on the body. In the Western world removal of hair from the body was not common until the 1920-ies. There are, of course, some countries, women are still not doing anything with their hair on the body. However, it is not worth talking about it right now.
The house in that period of time the ill-smelling natural feces of a human organism. Then there was no running water, and most of the people there were excreting in a pot, which might have been left until someone did not dare to throw out its contents to the nearest window. Later pots have been preserved in some of the houses, but had already been created by open toilets. However, even in the Victorian era, pots were used as emergency toilet in the night.
Toilet paper was invented in the late 18th century, and until then people used the materials at hand. The rich could afford the luxury of a piece of cloth to wipe yourself. Poor people used old rags, leaves, moss and ... hands! Even the ancient Romans were better - they are used for this purpose cloth wound around a stick, and then immerse it in a bucket of water.

original text:
La Aljaba is a children's home located at the southern most tip of Colombia in the city of Leticia, which resides along the Amazon River. We have been serving children and families of the Amazonas Region of Colombia for that last 15 years. .
We provide for children from 5 to 17 years of age in situations of extreme poverty or need. We offer assistance in four development areas: physical, cognitive, spiritual and emotional well-being. Currently we provide a full day care, tutoring, daily nutritional meals for 80 children and provide 24 hour care for 26 children. We also extend our reach to the community by providing family counseling and parenting classes monthly, sending daily food rations to 100 children who live in extreme poverty on the edge of the jungle, and helping other children's ministries 50 miles up and down the Amazon River.
​La Aljaba is able to continually move forward financially because of the help of our partner ministry, Orphanos. Orphanos is based in Memphis, Tennessee and helps raise awareness and financial needs to keep our ministry up and running.
La Aljaba Children's home is an institution that assists abandoned and impoverished children to improve their quality of life, by giving them the opportunity to benefit from integral development and to contribute to new generations with Christian principles and values.
La Aljaba is inspired by God's love and obeying His calling. "We will impact the Amazon community by teaching Christian values and principles to the children for future generations."
The triune nature of the Godhead; one God existing as Father, Son, and Holy Spirit; one in nature and unity, yet three in being.
The Deity of Jesus Christ; He is God Himself, and is eternally existed, possessing all power and knowledge; born into this world from the Virgin Mary by the Holy Spirit; is, at the same time, true God and true Man.
The Divine inspiration and inerrancy of the Bible, Old and New Testament; that the Bible is the divine Word of God and, in so being, is without error in its original text and is of supreme and final authority in faith and life.
Man was created in the image of God, sinned, and, thereby, was separated from God; each person born is separated from God by a sin nature with which he/she is born, and, in this condition, is unable by personal effort to be reconciled to God.
The Lord Jesus was crucified as an atonement for sins and, according to Scripture, served as the only possible substitute sacrifice able to sufficiently pay the penalty each human being otherwise must pay for his/her sins; those humans who place their faith in the fact that Jesus Christ served as the sacrifice which paid the penalty he/she should have paid for his/her sins, thereby, is reconciled to the living God and justified on the ground of Jesus Christ's sacrifice on the cross.
The third day after the death of Jesus Christ, He bodily arose from the dead, according to Scripture, for the new life of man, and He is now physically seated at the right-hand of God the Father as the High Priest and Advocate of all believers.
The imminent return to earth of the Jesus Christ to reign as Savior and Ruler of the Universe.
​The bodily resurrection of the dead, the just to eternal life with God, and the unjust to eternal separation from God.

original text:
The Strip Steel business unit is composed of the operating companies of Salzgitter Flachstahl GmbH, Salzgitter Bauelemente GmbH, Salzgitter Europlatinen GmbH and Salzgitter Mannesmann Stahlservice GmbH.
The Strip Steel Business Unit produces, processes and sells strip steel in a wide variety of metallurgic compositions and dimensions.
The largest company in the business unit is Salzgitter Flachstahl GmbH (SZFG) ,which operates one of the most state-of-the-art and environmentally-friendly integrated iron and steel works on the planet at its Salzgitter site. The facility produces almost 5 million tons of crude steel as well as around 3.5 million tons of rolled steel every year. The unavoidable carbon dioxide emissions generated in making steel from iron ore have been reduced down to near the technical minimum possible for this process, and substantially undercut the emissions of non-European competitors. The gases released during the production processes are captured for use in the company's own power plant, among other applications. As a result, the site covers its electricity requirements almost entirely in house and impresses with excellent energy efficiency.
Sustainability is a key element of our philosophy. SZFG has been repeatedly acknowledged by the German Energy Agency (dena) in recent years for its energy efficiency. This included winning the "Energy Efficiency Award" in 2013 and being awarded the "Best Practice Label" for energy-efficient pig iron production in 2014.
Our systems technology enables us to achieve the most minute tolerances in metallurgic compositions as well as dimensions - essential prerequisites for producing steels of the highest quality. In order to achieve this, we make targeted and continuous investments in our plant technology. Our strip steel is therefore ideally suited for tackling the most challenging applications across a range of customer sectors. The company's iron and steel works is one of the most efficient production sites in the European hot rolled strip sector and is one of only a handful of steel producers that can provide sheet in automobile body quality.
Over 65 % of SZFG's sales stem from the automobile sector - not just automobile manufacturers but also suppliers, cold rollers as well as steel service centers. Another 20 % of the company's products are purchased by industrial companies (e.g. household device producers and the mechanical engineering). The 8 % supplied to the tube industry is also of vital importance - from large-diameter pipes through to pipes for fuel injection systems. Approximately 3 % is supplied to the construction sector (roofing and walls). Within the Group, SZFG also supplies the plants within the Plate / Section Steel Business Unit with around 900,000 tons of slabs per year.
SZFG is a series supplier and development partner to technology leaders in its target segments. We strive to be leading innovators for steel products in the automobile chassis and bodywork sector as well as for cold rolling and household devices. In order to achieve and defend this position, we are committed to an intensive research and development cooperation program with Salzgitter Mannesmann Forschung GmbH as well as other partners within the industrial and academic arena.
One particular strength of the Strip Steel Business Unit is its high level of flexibility and the capacity to make rapid decisions in response to changes in customer demands. The flat hierarchies allow rapid and targeted processes - often in direct coordination with customers.
A portion of the strip steel volume produced in Salzgitter is processed by companies from within the business unit. Salzgitter Europlatinen GmbH, for instance, produces laser-welded blanks from various strip steel grades (tailored blanks) for the automobile industry, while Salzgitter Bauelemente GmbH makes profiled sheets and sandwich components for the construction industry.
The Strip Steel Business Unit is rounded out by the two steel service centers belonging to Salzgitter Mannesmann Stahlservice GmbH in Schwerte and Karlsruhe, offering comprehensive pre-processing options and a processing capacity of more than 600,000 tons per year. The branch office in Fellbach ensure the availability of service for customers nationwide.

original text:
Affordable glam; Who doesn't want that? At Technic they know what makes your heart beat faster. And that while they do not ask for sky-high prices, because Technic just wants to be available to everyone. Without compromising on quality, of course. It is no coincidence that Technic is one of the most popular make-up brands of the moment. Discover now what Technic Cosmetics has to offer and let yourself be enchanted by the extensive range!
Technic is a progressive make-up brand that originated in the United Kingdom. With ideals like cruelty free, it knows how to keep its values ​​high. In addition, the assortment consists of various vegetarian and vegan products, with which they have reduced animal interference to an absolute minimum. A cuddly brand, supported by products with a high wow factor. And these are available to everyone, to everyone's delight. Technic wants to be accessible to everyone at all times and you notice that at the prices they use. So very low budget! The range is extensive, so you never have to feel limited in freedom of choice. This way you can create a great make-up look for a super attractive price!
The make-up offer from Technic consists of a lot of options. What they have in common is that they let you all shine, and that for a price that makes you happy.
